_meta.description: Ontology of probability distributions with parameters, support, and typical usage for the Excel calc agent.

distributions.fittingMethod: Not fitted; used as reference distribution

typicalUse: Time between events in a Poisson process
Waiting times for multiple Poisson events
Bayesian conjugate prior for Poisson rate
Ratio of two independent standard normals
Confidence intervals when σ is unknown
Test of independence in contingency tables
Modeling differences of exponential random variables
Finance: modeling returns with heavy tails
Similar to normal but heavier tails
Extreme value analysis for heavy-tailed data

moments.mean: (1/b) · exp(η) · E₁(η) where E₁ is the exponential integral

typicalUse: First passage time of Brownian motion with drift

moments.mean: σ√(π/2) · L₁/₂(-ν²/(2σ²)) where L is Laguerre polynomial

typicalUse: Prior for standard deviation in Bayesian analysis

moments.mean: μ + σ(φ(α) - φ(β)) / (Φ(β) - Φ(α)) where α=(a-μ)/σ, β=(b-μ)/σ

typicalUse: Subjective prior when only min, mode, max known

moments.mean: bΒ(1 + 1/a, b) where Β is the beta function

typicalUse: Alternative to beta distribution with closed-form CDF
Simulation when inverse CDF sampling is needed
distributions.fittingMethod: MLE via custom implementation (no scipy built-in)

typicalUse: Modeling positive continuous data with heavy tails
Alternative to log-normal for heavy-tailed positive data
Number of successes in fixed number of trials
Count of events in a fixed interval
Number of trials until first success
Number of failures before r-th success
distributions.fittingMethod: MLE via scipy.stats.nbinom.fit or method of moments

typicalUse: Modeling count data with mode at 1

moments.mean: Σ k·exp(-λk) / Σ exp(-λk) for k=0..N-1

typicalUse: Bayesian compound distribution (binomial with beta prior on p)
Count data with under- or over-dispersion
distributions.fittingMethod: MLE via custom implementation or compoinger package

distributions.testForFit: Vuong test comparing ZIP vs. Poisson

typicalUse: Difference of two independent Poisson counts
distributions.fittingMethod: Method of moments: μ̂₁ = (s² + x̄)/2, μ̂₂ = (s² - x̄)/2
MLE: μ̂ = sample mean, Σ̂ = sample covariance
MLE via fixed-point iteration or Newton-Raphson

typicalUse: Bayesian conjugate prior for covariance matrices
distributions.fittingMethod: MLE: n̂V̂ = sample sum of outer products

typicalUse: Robust multivariate modeling with heavy tails
Financial risk modeling with tail dependence
Prior for correlation matrices in Bayesian models
distributions.pythonClass: pymc.LKJCorr or stan equivalent (not in scipy)
